row1.revenue: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a revenue of $716.9B USD, outpacing Walmart Inc. (WMT) by $3.7B USD. The top two companies hold 34.6% of the combined revenue of the top five.
row1.net_income: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with a Net Income of 132.2B USD in the latest fiscal year, outpacing its closest competitor NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) by 12.1B USD. A significant 30% gap separates GOOGL from the third-ranked AAPL.
row1.total_assets: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with $4.4T in Total Assets, a significant gap of $1T from Bank of America Corporation (BAC) in second place. This substantial gap underscores JPM's dominant position in the financial sector.
row1.total_equity: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with a staggering $415.3B in Total Equity, a significant $63.8B ahead of Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) in second place. This substantial gap underscores the immense financial strength of GOOGL.
row1.total_liabilities: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with Total Liabilities of 4.1T USD, a significant gap from the next closest Bank of America Corporation (BAC) with 3.1T USD in liabilities. This 1T USD gap underscores the substantial scale of JPM's balance sheet.
row1.cash_and_equivalents: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with $343.3B in Cash And Equivalents, a significant gap from Bank of America Corporation (BAC) with $239.3B. This 43% difference underscores JPM's substantial liquidity advantage.
row1.gross_profit: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a Gross Profit of $360.5B USD, a significant $120.2B USD gap from the second-place Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) at $240.3B USD.
row1.operating_income_ebit: AAPL leads the pack with a significant Operating Income Ebit of $133.1B USD, outpacing the next closest competitor by $3.9B USD. NVDA's strong Ebit of $130.4B USD is a close second, with a notable gap of $2.7B USD between the top two companies.

row2_sector.revenue: Consumer Cyclical and Technology sectors dominate revenue, accounting for 43.1% of the total, while the remaining sectors trail behind. Notably, the top two sectors combined make up nearly half of the total revenue.
row2_sector.total_assets: Financial Services sector dominates the industry with 64.1% of total assets, a significant concentration of wealth and influence. This dominance is further underscored by its substantial asset base of 8.1T USD.
row2_sector.net_income: Technology sector dominates the net income landscape with a 39.2% share, nearly doubling the next closest sector, Communication Services, which accounts for 24.8%. This significant concentration in Technology highlights the sector's substantial profitability.

row2_bubble: NVDA's 55.6% net margin is significantly higher than UNH's 2.7%, highlighting the stark contrast in profitability efficiency between these two companies, with NVDA leveraging its semiconductor expertise to achieve exceptional returns. This disparity underscores the importance of industry and business model in driving financial performance.
row3_heatmap: Morgan Stanley (MA) stands out for exceptional returns with a 193.5% ROE, while JPMorgan Chase (JPM) exhibits the highest leverage risk with a debt-to-equity ratio of 11.21x, suggesting a trade-off between profitability and financial stability.
row4_market_trends: Alphabet's (GOOGL) 12-month cumulative return of +107.6% leads the pack, significantly outperforming the market. Adobe (ADBE) takes the worst spot with a -48.6% return, a stark contrast to the top performer. Market volatility remains high, reflecting the wide disparity in stock performance.
